source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage - Mississippi Canyon 582 A (Medusa)

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 582

actions_taken: Murphy obtained quotes from vendors to remove crane boom from facility
Murphy committed to more thorough JSA descriptions and barricading requirements for future crane work
event.summary: On 16 April 2025 at approximately 1500 hours on the Medusa SPAR platform, a 15-pound cable roller (66 inches long, 1 inch diameter) broke off from an out-of-service crane boom and fell approximately 50 feet to the deck below, striking and damaging a hydraulic hose. The cable roller had major corrosion identified during the October 2022 annual crane inspection with recommendations to repair or replace within 1-3 months, but the repairs were never completed. No barricades were installed to prevent personnel from entering the drop zone beneath the crane during repositioning operations. No injuries occurred, but the incident had potential for serious injury or fatality.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Address corrosion issues discovered during annual crane inspections promptly
Install barricades or other means to prevent personnel from entering drop zone areas when work with dropped object potential is being performed
JSAs should provide more thorough description of possible dropped objects and require barricading the work area prior to performing work on cranes
Remove crane boom from facility to eliminate possibility of additional components falling
key_phrases: no barricades to block off the area
possibility of a serious injury or fatality

hazards.name: Corroded crane components under gravitational load

threats.name: Inadequate preventive maintenance of corroded cable rollers
Crane repositioning operations with degraded equipment

consequences.name: Personnel injury or fatality from falling object

controls.name: Annual crane inspection
controls.lod_basis: Routine inspection to identify hazards before failure

supporting_text: Upon review of the East crane annual inspection performed on 05 October 2022, it was noted that the boom had major corrosion on two of the boom mid-sections and major corrosion on the boom tip section
The crane inspector recommended that the cable rollers be replaced or repaired within one to three months on the annual crane inspection deficiency report
However, the parts were never ordered by Murphy to repair or replace the cable rollers

controls.name: Corrective maintenance following inspection findings
controls.barrier_role: Repair or replacement of degraded components
controls.lod_basis: Corrective action to restore equipment integrity after detection

supporting_text: The crane inspector recommended that the cable rollers be replaced or repaired within one to three months on the annual crane inspection deficiency report. However, the parts were never ordered by Murphy to repair or replace the cable rollers
Equipment failure: Inadequate preventive maintenance- The operator did not perform the recommended repairs that were noted on the annual crane inspection

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job planning to identify and control hazards

supporting_text: Both Murphy's cold work permit and Sparrows' JSA mentioned the potential danger of dropped objects while performing work on the crane
Supervision: Inadequate pre-job safety and operation planning- Failure to properly identify the need to secure the area where the potential for dropped object were present on the Sparrows' JSA

controls.name: Cold Work Permit
controls.barrier_role: Work authorization and hazard control verification
controls.lod_basis: Permit-to-work system to ensure controls are in place

supporting_text: Both Murphy's cold work permit and Sparrows' JSA mentioned the potential danger of dropped objects while performing work on the crane

Supervision: Inadequate pre-job safety and operation planning- Failure to properly identify the need to secure the area where the potential for dropped object were present on the Sparrows' JSA, Murphy's Cold Work Permit

controls.name: Working at Heights Permit
controls.barrier_role: Authorization and control for elevated work
controls.lod_basis: Permit system to control work at height hazards

supporting_text: As per Murphy's Working at Heights Permit, the only barricades that were installed on the platform were those that were protecting open hole locations

Supervision: Inadequate pre-job safety and operation planning- Failure to properly identify the need to secure the area where the potential for dropped object were present on Murphy's Working at Heights Permit

controls.name: Drop zone barricades
controls.barrier_role: Physical exclusion of personnel from drop zone
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent personnel exposure to dropped objects

supporting_text: However, it was noted by the operator that there were no barricades to block off the area underneath the crane to prevent personnel from traveling under the crane while the work was being performed
By not having the barricades installed around the working area under the crane, it allowed personnel to be within 20 feet of the cable roller when it fell to the deck
Human Performance Error: Not aware of hazards- Failure to secure the area underneath the crane prior to starting operations and preventing personnel from entering the hazard areas

controls.name: Crane out-of-service status
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent use of degraded equipment
controls.lod_basis: Operational restriction to prevent use of unsafe equipment

supporting_text: Per Murphy, the East Crane was placed out of service on 12 January 2022 and was still out of service at the time of the incident
The crane mechanics were in the process of removing the main winch and the auxiliary winch to have them refurbished and use as spares on other facilities
Murphy also stated that the East Crane was not intended to be placed back in service

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
